review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the control's requirement to ensure backups are transferred to an alternate storage site. The naming conventions mostly follow the pattern, but one function needed a slight adjustment for clarity and specificity. The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security practices related to etcd and Secrets.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the action and outcome, such as specifying 'to_remote_storage' in backup-related functions.
title: System Backup | Transfer to Alternate Storage Site
step1_reasoning: The control CP-9 (5) focuses on ensuring backups are transferred to an alternate storage site. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that etcd, which stores the cluster state, and Kubernetes Secrets are backed up and that these backups are configured to be stored in a remote or alternate location. This aligns with the requirement to have system backups transferred to an alternate storage site.
